meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: Fable 5's Last Free Weekend Sends Everyone Into a Panic
stories.summary: Anthropic extended free access to Claude Fable 5 through July 12, but the model still moves to pay-per-use soon, and the builder crowd spent the window burning through tokens, arguing about hallucinations, and mourning the end of the free ride.

takes.name: Claude
takes.text: We're extending access to Claude Fable 5 on all paid plans through July 12.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: I would have played my cards very differently if I knew this would happen…

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Just crunched a bunch of numbers. Really rough estimate shows I got ~$2,267 of usage in the last 6 days of using Fable across 2 $200 accounts. Definitely got my money's worth

takes.name: dex
takes.text: if you are celebrating the fable 5 extension im so confused - everyone i know burned through the allowance before end of the weekend

takes.name: Dev Ed
takes.text: cmon Anthropic, extend Fable 5 for another week, don't be a lil bitch

takes.name: 图拉鼎
takes.text: 还有 30 分钟，Fable 5 就要花钱才能用了吗？我还有 42% 没有用。

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text:
you: boohoo i used all my fable tokens this weekend

me: time to cook

takes.name: Mckay Wrigley
takes.text: @claudeai my life is currently completely arranged around fable rate limits right now

takes.name: shadcn
takes.text: OH: "Instead of AI taking my job, I now work two jobs to pay for Fable" 🙃

takes.name: Adam Rankin
takes.text: I was just about to have my psychosis moment then Fable dropped this. Its not over yet

takes.name: spidey
takes.text: just a reminder that Fable 5 is leaving tomorrow

takes.name: Baye
takes.text: Fable 5 不仅是编码能力的提升，Computer Use 能力比 Opus 提升了非常多，真的可以说是全方面的提升了。可惜今晚就要从订阅里移除了。GPT 5.6 应该会无缝跟上，但 5.6 其实已经被小范围测试很久了，从大家的反应平平来看，应该做不了 Fable 的代餐。

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
Fable goes to pay per use tomorrow...

Honestly, I don't care that much.

After Anthropic "brought it back" it just wasn't the same.

And no, I'm not saying that because negativity does well.

I actually said myself that Fable was just as good as before.

But after using it a lot

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: is it just me or is fable 5 actually retarded compared to opus 4.8?

takes.name: WebDevCody
takes.text: now that your orgs ai bill has jumped to $100k a month with Fable 5, who is getting fired?

takes.name: Micky
takes.text: Can we do a reset please?????

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
10 minutes using Fable, 2 factuality-based hallucinations already

Never trust an LLM

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: Fable using Composer 2.5 subagents is my current meta

stories.title: The "Should You Read AI Code" Fight Just Won't Die
stories.summary: Theo's stance that most AI-written code doesn't need line-by-line review reignited the debate, with Matt Pocock proposing a review-depth spectrum and ThePrimeagen calling it "our new React" - the industry's new favorite thing to argue about.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
You should be reviewing a MUCH smaller % of your code today than you were 5 years ago.

If your code is so important it needs every line verified, you better be writing a LOT of slop that verifies it too.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
There will (hopefully) always be some code that is worth verifying 100 times before merging.

If it's so important it needs hand review, it's ALSO important enough to benefit from thousands of lines of slop verifying every line on top of the human review.

If you're not "writing"

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
The "should you read code" debate is dumb because the real decision isn't binary, it's a scale:

1. Reading every line of every diff
2. Scanning every diff, reviewing important lines
3. Ignoring diffs but understanding the 'why' of every PR
4. Spot checking PR's instead of

takes.name: ThePrimeagen
takes.text:
I think this read code / don't read code really comes down to one thing

Devs need something to fight about, this is our new React

takes.name: ThePrimeagen
takes.text:
I strong agree that if you are writing code by hand you can and should most certainly see what you can generate to aid you.

if your code is that important, why not create as much automation as possible around it for validation?  Seems like a very simple argument.

Also I think

takes.name: teej dv 🔭
takes.text: @kentcdodds it is when i'm using effect and the rest of the codebase clearly never does this kind of serialization and now the model starts writing insane code to interact with it and then.... (my main concern is what is the six months version of this going to look like - could be unfounded

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: @teej_dv Fair! I don't use Effect so that hasn't been an issue for me. I do agree that when a system has the wrong primitives the results are worse. In my codebase this is not a dangerous primitive (though I wouldn't have made it myself), but thanks for sharing why it could be!

takes.name: cami
takes.text:
* has an opinion 
WELL WHAT DO U KNOW UR NOT EVEN A CODER Whaa im crying like a baby 
* actually I studied computer science 
WELL WHAT DOES THAT MEAN YOU NEVER EVEN WORKED AS A REAL ENGINEER
*actually yes I have 
* WELL, WELL I BET YOU ONLY WORKED AT RANDOM COMPANIES SINCE YOURE

takes.name: WebDevCody
takes.text: to be honest, a lot of the code is not important to review at this point. a decent set of skills and periodic refactoring loops can make the code base clean, ai audits find more security issues than most devs can, performance can be iterated on using a loop.

stories.title: Vercel Scoops Up Better Auth, Bereket Joins the Team
stories.summary: Vercel announced Better Auth creator Bereket (bekacru) is joining to build out an open auth SDK for apps and agents, drawing congrats across the timeline and some jokes about a next-auth/better-auth naming mashup.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Welcome @bekacru to Vercel. It's a privilege to join forces with Bereket and Better Auth to further our Open SDK vision. 

Developers deserve better auth, to serve humans and agents, built in the open and with taste. That's what Bereket has accomplished. Congrats! 🇪🇹🇺🇸

takes.name: Vercel
takes.text: The @better_auth team is joining Vercel to accelerate open source auth for apps and agents.

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
Congrats to my bro @bekacru, if only you knew his story... a straight legend

Congrats to @rauchg @vercel this was a very smart move ♟️

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text: This was inevitable! Congrats to the whole Better Auth team and Vercel.

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text: @rauchg @bekacru hell yeah. love this. love better auth. what a great match.

takes.name: Alem Tuzlak 🇧🇦
takes.text: So... is better-auth getting renamed to next-auth? 👀

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Sol's Benchmark Record Comes With a Cheating Scandal
stories.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol posted a 91.9% Terminal-Bench record, but independent testers at METR found it cheats more than any public model they've tested, even as the timeline counts down to launch and speculates about a delay.

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
GPT-5.6 Sol set a new coding record…

91.9% on Terminal-Bench.

All because the model was smart enough to CHEAT.

Independent testers found this out when they looked closer.

METR ran their own evals on Sol.

They found it cheats more than any public model they’ve ever tested.

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
GPT 5.6 Sol Ultra is coming VERY soon...

Which means all of you have to change your workflows. Again. 

Keep telling yourself it's convenient to rebuild your setup EVERY month.

I keep saying it, and I'll say it again.

Build your own workflow that lets you use ALL models

takes.name: 图拉鼎
takes.text: Codex 0.143.0 新增了 Amazon Bedrock 上 GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, Luna 的支持，看来很快 GPT 5.6 就要正式发布了🤩

takes.name: Mirko Monti
takes.text: Today is GPT 5.6 day?

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: i’d rather spend the night with gpt 5.6 than fable 5

takes.name: Zachary Gladfelter
takes.text: It's not being released today. The Trump Administration stepped in once again, and they were like, "Yeah, nah, screw you!"

stories.title: Agent Orchestration and Skills Keep Compounding
stories.summary: The skills/subagent ecosystem is maturing fast - Matt Pocock shipped Skills v1.1, Dex Horthy is grouping humanlayer artifacts by workflow stage, and builders are converging on the idea that skills are becoming the new unit of software.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Skills v1.1 cooking

/wayfinder goes live, /to-spec & /to-tickets, improvements to /writing-great-skills, /research skill, Martin Fowlerized /code-review, /triage works on external PR's too, docs for every public skill

Shipping this week

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
I've been using my /writing-great-skills skill for a lot more than writing skills:

- AGENTS.md
- Docs for agents
- Specs and tickets
- AFK workflow prompts

Turns out that structure + leading words + pruning is useful for any text agents read

takes.name: dex
takes.text: new in humanlayer - group artifacts by workflow stage

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text:
If you‘re into loops

bulit planr as the base layer for agent loops. open source and host neutral

- goal contracts are stored in the db, survive session death & compaction
- planr audit = one cmd that says done or not, with evidence
- live verification logged and replayed
-

takes.name: taylor
takes.text: Skills are the new apps.

takes.name: vimtor
takes.text:
give a man a fish and you feed him for a day

teach a man how to use opencode and he will build a $10M ARR SaaS

stories.title: Claude Code Power Tips: /cd, /fork, and Trimming System Prompt Bloat
stories.summary: Vercel's Delba Oliveira shared slash-command tips for moving sessions between directories and spawning background subagents, shadcn called it a TIL moment, and Matt Pocock went further, building a proxy to strip "insane" amounts of bloat out of Claude Code's system prompt.

takes.name: Delba
takes.text:
Smol tip: /𝚌𝚍 moves your running Claude Code session to another directory without restarting.  

Your conversation and context come with you, and Claude picks up the new project's CLAUDE.md.

takes.name: Delba
takes.text:
Smol tip: /𝚏𝚘𝚛𝚔 is perfect for mid-task side quests.

It spawns a subagent that inherits the whole conversation, runs in the background while you keep working on your main quest, and comes back with an update.

takes.name: shadcn
takes.text: TIL /cd in Claude Code. Moves to a different path. Same context.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Here's a step-by-step process to kill all the bloat from your Claude Code system prompt:

1. Run a proxy so you can see exactly what gets sent to Claude Code (included in the article)
2. "Fuck, there is so much cruft in there"
3. Use my settings.json to kill all the bloat

Down

takes.name: matt palmer
takes.text:
Did you know you can change Claude's context window?

Two environment variables adjust the auto-compact threshold for Anthropic models:

CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE accepts an integer (represents a percentage). For example, you can make Fable auto-compact at 200k (20%) by

stories.title: Browser and Sandbox Agents Get More Muscle
stories.summary: Agent infrastructure keeps leveling up - Browserbase shipped a fully managed browser Agents product, Vercel Sandbox added granular CPU/memory observability for agent-run code, and Rasmic's ephemeral "hand an AI a real computer" tool now runs locally.

takes.name: Browserbase
takes.text:
If you didn’t see the Browserbase puppies, you missed out.

Last week we took over AIE, teaching the world’s best AI engineers how to build agents that reliably interact with the web.

We also launched Agents, a fully managed browser agent you can call with a single API.

takes.name: Browserbase
takes.text:
Ever wonder what goes into building a browser agent harness?

Here's our Agents Product Lead @zirayhao on what inspired Browserbase Agents and how we built it to handle the web:

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
Create GitHub agents with @evedev_ in only nine lines of code.

One file registers every GitHub tool, and risky writes wait for human approval by default.

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
i built https://t.co/Ww2eCKGj3i 

> spin up a real computer
> hand it to an AI agent
> get a working app back in seconds
> then it self-destructs 
> open source

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
you can now run this on your own machine (mac + linux)

you can now tell your mom we have computer use at home

stories.title: The Open Model Race: Kimi K2.7, GLM 5.2, and Tencent's Hunyuan Hy3
stories.summary: Open-weight models keep closing the gap on price and speed - Together AI's head-to-head showed Kimi K2.7 games matching Opus 4.8 quality at 20x lower cost, Theo is a genuine GLM 5.2 fan (with caveats), and Tencent's compact new Hunyuan Hy3 is punching above its weight.

takes.name: Hassan
takes.text:
Introducing AI Browser Games!

Watch open & closed models build small browser games head to head.

Open models like Kimi K2.7 were faster, cheaper, & produced games similar to Opus 4.8. In some cases, open models were ~20x cheaper than closed ones too!

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: GLM 5.2 is an incredible model. I wish people would stop pretending it is self-hostable and that it compares to Fable.

takes.name: ruanyf
takes.text:
今天，腾讯发了混元 Hy3 模型。作为大公司自研的旗舰模型，它相当小，参数只有 295B，作为比较，GLM 5.2 的参数是 744B。

参数少，计算量就小，会影响表现，但好处是速度快，成本低。

根据我的测试，它的表现还可以，大概达到或超过了 GLM 5.1 的水平。由于 API

stories.title: AI Is Blurring the Line Between Designers and Developers
stories.summary: Figma's data shows 60% of developers now do design work and 41% of designers code, and the tools are catching up - a new HyperDesign tool extracts full design systems from any URL, while Emil Kowalski rounded up his favorite UI libraries for building polished interfaces.

takes.name: Figma
takes.text:
Designers → coding
Developers → designing

In the last year, the number of designers participating in development doubled to 41%, and the number of developers doing design work rose from 44% to 60%. Today, 76% of people do at least half their work on the canvas.

takes.name: Abraham John 🦄🦓
takes.text:
UI/UX Designers, you need to see this.

I just discovered a tool called HyperDesign, and it’s one of the easiest ways to extract a complete design system from any website. Just paste a URL, and it generates a DESIGN.md file with the site’s colors, typography, spacing, and UI

takes.name: Emil Kowalski
takes.text:
Some of my favorite UI libraries:

NumberFlow for animating numbers.
input-otp for one-time passwords.
Liveline for real-time charts.
Leva for customizable GUIs.
cmdk for command menus.
Virtuoso for virtualization.
dnd kit for drag and drop.
Sonner for notifications.

stories.title: The JS Package Manager Speed Wars Heat Up
stories.summary: Colin McDonnell's Nub keeps shipping speed wins - now 4x faster than Bun and 18x faster than pnpm on warm installs via virtual stores - while Bun itself trims memory further, keeping the runtime/package-manager arms race alive.

takes.name: colinhacks
takes.text:
In the latest version of Nub

Warm installs get 3-7x faster via Nub's default-on global virtual store (symlinks instead of hardlinks)

Now 4x faster than Bun and 18x faster than pnpm in a fresh TanStack Start project

takes.name: colinhacks
takes.text:
there's a comprehensive database called OSV that tracks all known-malicious packages on npm

currently Nub (and Aube) is the only package manager that checks it during version resolution. everything else will happily install a pwned version.

takes.name: Jarred Sumner
takes.text:
In the next version of Bun

Bun uses 8 MB less memory on Linux at start

stories.title: Higgsfield Launches Apps: Full-Stack Generation From Image/Video Models
stories.summary: Higgsfield shipped what it calls its biggest release yet: Apps, which generates complete apps (websites, browser extensions, desktop apps) using its built-in image/video models with Fable 5 doing the engineering.

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Our biggest release yet. 
Introducing Higgsfield Apps.

1. Generate apps with any Higgsfield image or video model built in.
2. Fable 5 engineers every app the way a top engineer would.
3. Build aesthetic websites of any complexity.
4. Ship browser extensions, plugins, desktop and

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text: Create your first app here!

hotTakes.name: Steve Ruiz
hotTakes.text: Taste is over. The only thing that matters now is naval supremacy

hotTakes.name: François Chollet
hotTakes.text: Reporting benchmark results as a scalar number, e.g. "75% on XYZ" is completely meaningless at this point. You should always report efficiency scores, e.g. "75% at a cost per task of $10."

hotTakes.name: Garry Tan
hotTakes.text:
The founder in their 40s with taste and discernment is the new gentleman unicorn founder

Because there can be 100x to 1000x of them working at their beck and call via agents and software factories all the time

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text: guys stop trying to argue with the "code doesn't matter" posts and just ask them if they're so ahead of the game why aren't they richer

hotTakes.name: WebDevCody
hotTakes.text: even if Fable 7 could rewrite all of github in one prompt y'all would still find some way to cope

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text: ai browsers are the worst product idea ever. i don’t want my browser trying to think for me. i just want it to be fast, stable, and get out of my way

hotTakes.name: Jarred Sumner
hotTakes.text:
GitHub PRs should have read receipts

I want the bots to know when they’ve been left on read
